signal.snapshotStale: Signal data has not updated for {days} days — the backend may be down; do not treat the current tier as up to date
signal.capexGuidanceBanner: Hyperscaler capex guidance cut — forward signal of capex contraction & AI oversupply; AI supply/demand turned TIGHT

hints.modelUsageTrend: Model usage trend: trailing 28-day average volume vs the prior 28-day average (monthly-level smoothing that dilutes single-week holiday noise). >+3% (roughly ≥+47% annualized growth) → strong demand · loose; <−3% (sustained decline in the monthly average) → weakening demand · oversupply warning · tight. Source: openrouter.ai/rankings — a single-aggregator proxy subject to share noise.
hints.capexYoY: Rule: hyperscaler (MSFT/AMZN/GOOGL/META) rolling-4Q capex YoY >+10% → loose vote (investment boom); <0% → tight vote (capex contraction · oversupply warning — any tight link in the trio makes AI supply/demand tight). Quarterly scout rules (2026-07-20): a negative latest single quarter blocks a loose vote (neutral at most); two straight negative quarters → tight directly, without waiting for TTM to turn (1-2 quarters earlier). 2nd layer of the cash-flow trio (investment side): model providers buy cloud compute
hints.capexQtrYoY: Limited judgment role (since 2026-07-20): YoY of the latest calendar quarter that all four have reported (fiscal-calendar aligned — no mixed quarters mid earnings season). The single quarter is the turning-point scout — TTM is a 4-quarter moving average that dilutes a brake and lags 2-3 reporting seasons. Two rules: ① a negative quarter blocks the capex sub-signal from voting loose (precedent: balance-sheet QT only blocks loose); ② two straight negative quarters → capex votes tight directly, without waiting for TTM. Badge marks this row's own state at the TTM thresholds (>+10% loose / <0% tight); the divergence moment when the quarterly badge turns before TTM is the earliest turning-point alert.
hints.semiIpYoy: Rule: semiconductor output YoY >+5% → loose vote; <0% → tight vote. Semi output is the supply-side tail of the AI supply/demand cash-flow trio (usage → cloud capex → semi output). Trio consensus rule: any link contracting → tight (oversupply warning); the whole chain rising → loose (undersupply); otherwise neutral.
hints.rate: Judged by the direction of the latest FOMC decision: a hike (incl. gradual 25bp) → tight, held until the next decision; a cut or hold → loose. A single adjustment ≥50bp (either direction) additionally triggers the reactive adjustment lock, forcing full defense.
hints.balanceSheet: Balance sheet change (WoW): expansion >0.25% (QE) → loose vote; contraction >0.25% (QT) does not read tight on its own — it only blocks a loose rating (backtest: markets rose in most QT years); in between → pause.
hints.sahm: Rule: Sahm value ≥0.5% → early recession, triggers the Sahm lock forcing full defense (<0.5% does not trigger). Unlock: rate down to ≤0.25% (zero-rate zone — unconditional unlock, even with the Sahm value still elevated, not subject to the minimum lock age); or a <50bp small rate adjustment — requiring both that the Sahm value has fallen back below 0.5% that day (trigger gone) and the lock is at least 60 days old (minimum lock age)
hints.yieldCurve: 10-year minus 3-month US Treasury yield. Normally positive (lending longer should earn more); negative = inversion. Why it works: (1) the 10-year yield reflects expected future short rates, so inversion means the bond market is pricing in "the Fed will cut hard = recession"; (2) banks borrow short and lend long — inversion makes lending unprofitable, credit contracts, and recession becomes self-fulfilling. Every US recession since 1968 (8 of 8) was preceded by a sustained inversion, zero misses, leading by 12-18 months on average. Because the lead is long (markets rallied 15 more months after the 2006 inversion; the 2022 inversion was followed by the AI bull), it is unfit as an exit signal — its only role here: when inverted for ≥3 months (63 trading days) it vetoes entry into the attack tier (downgraded to neutral; no maximum leverage while the recession clock is confirmed ticking). It never triggers defense and is not a lock.
hints.epuTrade: Rule: trade policy uncertainty index (monthly, trade/tariff/tech-war specific) 10-year percentile >80 → tight, <50 → loose; must agree with daily EPU, else neutral
hints.reference: Reference only, not part of signal judgment
hints.epuDaily: Rule: daily economic policy uncertainty index 7-day MA (news-based; reacts within days to policy pivots like tariff pauses) 10-year percentile >80 → tight, <50 → loose; must agree with the monthly trade index, else neutral
hints.oilWti: WTI 30-day change is the market's real-time pricing of war/geopolitical shocks, taking priority over EPU percentiles: gain ≥+20% AND EPU elevated (>80th pct) → tight (war/supply shock); a surge while EPU is calm = demand recovery, not misread as defense. Drop ≥20% AND EPU not elevated → loose (de-escalation); if EPU is simultaneously high the crash is crisis-type — no loose. Oil-level guard (2026-07-19): a surge only counts as a war shock if the oil price is also at a high level (above its 2-year median) — low-base rebounds right after crises (e.g. $45 in 2009, $38 in 2020) happen while EPU is inevitably still elevated and are not war shocks; high-level surges (e.g. $110 during the 2022 Russia-Ukraine war) are unaffected.
hints.fiscalOutlaysTtm: Rule (small-government): TTM federal outlays YoY expands >5% → tight (government growing, harmful to markets); shrinks >5% → loose (government slimming). Why outlays not deficit: deficit mixes in the revenue side — tax-cut-driven deficits (e.g. 2017) fit the low-tax principle and should not read as tight. Measured as real (inflation-adjusted) outlays YoY: nominal outlays naturally grow ~5% and would chronically pre-trigger tight; after PCE deflation it only fires when real expansion/contraction exceeds ±5%

indicators.hintGlobal: Global rule: Attack (asymmetric) = AI supply/demand loose (the active engine firing) + monetary/fiscal/admin all not tight (neutral is fine; any tight vetoes) + no locks + US Treasury yield curve not in confirmed inversion (10y−3m inverted ≥3 months / 63 trading days vetoes attack entry, downgrading to watch); one dimension tight = reduce & watch — stop adding, raise alertness; no need to trim existing positions (backtest evidence: months with a single tight dimension average market returns no lower than other months — a single tight is an attention signal, not an action signal); two+ tight or Sahm/reactive lock active = full defense. Trend re-entry exception: when the market is in an uptrend (latest close ≥ SMA of the last 10 month-end closes), decision-tree-driven full defense is downgraded to reduce & watch; lock-driven defense is never overridden by the trend. Downgrade hysteresis: upgrades (more defensive, incl. lock-forced) take effect immediately; downgrades (more lenient) require the milder tier to persist for a 30-day confirmation period, keeping the previous tier meanwhile Two refinements (2026-07-18): a pure monetary+fiscal double-tight (no administrative dim, no lock) only reaches Reduce; Sahm-lock-driven defense is likewise downgraded to Reduce in an uptrend (the reactive-adjustment lock is never overridden by trend).
indicators.staleHint: Data source temporarily unavailable; last valid judgment retained

watchlist.title: Watchlist
watchlist.percentileHint: Position of current price within all closes of the selected range. Rule: ≥80% high (red, euphoria — be cautious / consider selling); ≤20% low (green, neglected — watch for buying); 20–80% mid (yellow, wait). 99% = near range high, 10% = near range low
watchlist.valuationHint: ETFs/indexes have no financial statements, so blank P/E and P/S is normal; for individual stocks, blanks usually mean a temporary data-source rate limit — refresh later

interpret.title: Signal Interpretation
interpret.toAttack: Toward attack (AI supply/demand loose + monetary/fiscal/admin not tight + no locks + US Treasury yield curve not in confirmed inversion):
interpret.allLoose: Attack conditions met: AI supply/demand loose (active engine firing), monetary/fiscal/admin not tight, no locks, US Treasury yield curve not in confirmed inversion
interpret.yieldCurveOk: US Treasury yield curve not in confirmed inversion

recessionLock.waitCondition: Unlock: rate down to 0-0.25% (zero-rate zone — unconditional, not subject to the minimum lock age); or a <50bp small rate adjustment once the lock is at least 60 days old and the trigger condition has cleared that day

admin.title: Admin Panel
admin.lockOverrideNote: Emergency valve: manually clear if data anomaly caused a false trigger
admin.capexGuidanceHint: Event-type input: tier locked to tight. Record when a hyperscaler explicitly cuts capex guidance on an earnings call (note which company/what was said); AI supply/demand tightens immediately and subscribers are emailed. EDGAR actuals lag 1-2 reporting seasons — this event is the system's only forward-looking manual input. Set an expiry or clear manually when the event lapses.

track.title: Public Signal Track Record
track.subtitle: Daily signal tiers are archived here immutably for anyone to verify. Backtest methodology and full report: docs/backtest-report.md in the GitHub repo. For research reference only, not investment advice.
track.stratCagr: Strategy CAGR (exit on full defense only)
track.btNote: Based on a {years}-year monthly-replay backtest; past performance does not guarantee future results.

s5.title: S5 Execution Desk
s5.disclaimer: Admin only · Personal reference · Not investment advice

action.sell_all: ⚡ Today: sell ALL TQQQ holdings to cash; new contributions go to the reserve
action.buyback_all: ⚡ Today: buy back TQQQ in full, all at once (buy back even when recovering only to Reduce — this is S5's lifeline)
action.stay_cash: Stay in cash; this month's contribution goes to the cash reserve while waiting for Defense to lift
action.hold_accumulate: Hold the existing position; this month's contribution goes to the cash reserve (no buying during Reduce)
action.hold_deploy: Hold; buy TQQQ with this month's contribution plus the entire cash reserve

s5.downgradePending: Downgrade pending: the decision tree has turned looser; confirmation period until {date} (+30 days)
s5.capeActive: CAPE {cape} (30y percentile P{pct}) > 90 → TQQQ target weight 55%
